title: swe:recent-commit-bug-hunt Synthetic Review Dataset
description: Synthetic seed review items for swe:recent-commit-bug-hunt. Use these for UI smoke tests and rubric walk-throughs only.

items.title: Last 24 hours with local git and validation available (likely pass)
items.userRequest: Review my commits from the last 24 hours and tell me what I probably broke, with the smallest safe fixes.

notes: Git history, diffs, and targeted local validation are available.
The report should rank only evidence-backed findings.

sharedCriteria.title: Concrete Evidence
sharedCriteria.description: Claims are grounded in visible artifacts such as files, commits, CI output, metrics, or commands.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: Important claims cite specific evidence from the available sources.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The response relies on generic advice or unsupported assertions.

sharedCriteria.title: Actionable Output
sharedCriteria.description: The response leaves the user with clear next steps, a usable report, or a concrete decision.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: The output is directly usable for follow-on work or review.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The output is vague, purely descriptive, or does not help the next decision.

sharedCriteria.title: Instruction Adherence
sharedCriteria.description: The response follows the skill's hard constraints and workflow guidance.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: It respects the skill's do-not-use rules, evidence bar, and requested output shape.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: It violates explicit skill rules or skips required parts of the workflow.

sharedCriteria.title: Reviewable Structure
sharedCriteria.description: The output is organized so a reviewer can quickly inspect evidence, findings, and recommendations.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: The structure makes it easy to review decisions and trace them back to evidence.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The response is hard to audit because evidence, findings, and recommendations are mixed together.

reviewQuestions: Does each proposed issue clearly tie back to one or more recent commits?
Does the report avoid generic cleanup ideas and stay behavior-focused?
Does every proposed remediation include a narrow validation path?

passConditions: Names the exact commit or timeframe under review.
Ties findings to concrete repo evidence such as diffs, files, CI, or tests.
Separates strong or moderate findings from weak signals.
Recommends only the smallest behavior-focused follow-up sessions.
failConditions: Invents bugs with no commit-linked evidence.
Turns weak suspicion into a fix plan.
Broadens into generic code review or cleanup advice.
Suggests refactors instead of minimal remediation sessions.
commonFailureModes: Reporting old issues not clearly connected to the scoped commits.
Forgetting to list the validation path for a proposed fix.
Collapsing evidence strength into a single undifferentiated list.
Overusing broad test runs unrelated to the changed surface.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scope reviewed
- Last 24 hours of commits by the requested author

## Ranked findings
1. Likely regression in review packet formatting
   Evidence: the packet renderer originally truncated multi-line descriptions; the issue reproduced locally when rendering `repo-introspection`.
   Minimal fix: adjust frontmatter parsing and re-run the packet command.
   Validation: `npm run evals:packet -- repo-introspection`

## Weak-signal findings skipped
- A possible docs inconsistency in review-data guidance looked suspicious, but there is no failing example tied to a recent commit yet.

## Proposed remediation session
- Session name: fix review packet description parsing
- Target surface: `scripts/render-review-packet.mjs`
- Validation: rerender one packet and confirm the full description appears.

artifacts.title: Static checks
content: Findings cite specific commits or changed files.
Weak signals are separated from fix proposals.

reference.notes: Synthetic seed output intended to satisfy most rubric expectations.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scoped Readout
- User request: Review my commits from the last 24 hours and tell me what I probably broke, with the smallest safe fixes.
- Reviewed first: `Commit history`, `Diffs`, `Local validation`, `Optional CI status`

## swe:recent-commit-bug-hunt Draft
1. Scoped commit list: tie this to Commit history, then stop at the requested boundary.
2. Ranked findings: tie this to Diffs, then stop at the requested boundary.
3. Minimal remediation sessions: tie this to Local validation, then stop at the requested boundary.

## Labeled Unknowns
- Git history, diffs, and targeted local validation are available.
- The report should rank only evidence-backed findings.

## Smallest Next Step
- Use this draft as the narrowest reviewable starting point, then validate only the touched surface.

- Use this draft as the narrowest reviewable starting point, then validate only the touched surface.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scope reviewed
- Last 24 hours of commits by the requested author

## Ranked findings
1. Likely regression in review packet formatting
   Evidence: the packet renderer originally truncated multi-line descriptions; the issue reproduced locally when rendering `repo-introspection`.
   Minimal fix: adjust frontmatter parsing and re-run the packet command.
   Validation: `npm run evals:packet -- repo-introspection`

## Weak-signal findings skipped
- A possible docs inconsistency in review-data guidance looked suspicious, but there is no failing example tied to a recent commit yet.

## Proposed remediation session
- Session name: fix review packet description parsing
- Target surface: `scripts/render-review-packet.mjs`
- Validation: rerender one packet and confirm the full description appears.
